easy.companyDescription: An internet retailer expanding quickly across consumer categories.
easy.macroContext: Internet valuations are peaking as investors start demanding proof of durable profits.
easy.situation: Will scale turn heavy spending into a lasting advantage, or will cash burn collide with tighter capital?
easy.longCase: Revenue is growing rapidly as more shoppers move online, and category expansion could lock in a durable position.
easy.shortCase: Losses are mounting while the stock has already run far ahead of current earnings, making a sentiment reversal costly.
setupHints: Fulfillment, marketing, and expansion spending are rising faster than near-term profit.

medium.companyDescription: An online commerce company growing faster than its profits.
medium.macroContext: The market is starting to separate internet businesses with real economics from pure speculation.
medium.situation: Is this a scale story that compounds, or a cash-burn story that fails when funding tightens?
medium.longCase: Scale from online demand could eventually turn heavy spending into a lasting advantage.
medium.shortCase: Cash burn and an inflated valuation become brutal when capital gets tighter.

hard.macroContext: Speculative growth stocks are under increasing scrutiny near the end of a market boom.
hard.situation: Does prioritizing scale over near-term profit build a durable position or invite collapse?
hard.longCase: A fast-growing online business could entrench itself if demand keeps compounding.
hard.shortCase: If investors stop funding losses, the growth model can break quickly.

reveal.shortText: That was Amazon. The dot-com crash wiped out most of its market value, but it survived to become one of the most valuable companies in the world.
reveal.funFact: Amazon stock fell from about $107 to $7 a share during the bust — a roughly 93% decline.
whyItMoved: Internet valuations collapsed as capital tightened.
Investors demanded a path to profit instead of pure growth.
The stock had already priced aggressive expansion success.

sources.label: Prototype sample seed
sources.notes: Placeholder sample content for Phase 3 pipeline; not production research.

review.reviewNotes: Prototype/sample seed for Phase 3 schema pipeline. Approximate prices and returns; not Gate 1/Gate 2 production content.
